search_queries_used: DHA telehealth fever in children clinical guideline PDF
population_applicability: Direct for children with fever; the baseline viral-fever diagnosis label is not assumed from the presentation.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact source is limited to DHA-licensed telehealth services.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai and DHA-licensed telehealth only; broader UAE applicability is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official Version/Issue 2 copy opened on 2026-07-11; document states revision date 2029-02-21.
superseded_check: DHA official telehealth portal checked on 2026-07-11; no newer official issue was identified there.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports pediatric fever terminology, age, temperature, appearance, persistence, and underlying-condition context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines pediatric fever assessment context and the importance of age, measurement site, appearance, persistence, and underlying disease.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Defines the exact Dubai pediatric telehealth scope and emergency exclusions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines Dubai telehealth pediatric fever scope and emergency exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, feeding, activity, urine, hydration, vital-sign, and associated-symptom documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Prioritises airway, breathing, circulation, consciousness, age-specific risk, and illness-severity assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports mapped high-risk concern prompts and clinician-documented safety-net or follow-up decisions.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Uses risk features to guide emergency referral, specialist assessment, or explicitly documented safety-netting.

unresolved_source_gaps: The baseline viral-fever diagnosis label and viral aliases remain unsupported unless clinician-stated.
Medication, antibiotic, hydration, sick-note, and fixed follow-up content is not promoted by this mapping.
The workflow is broader than DHA telehealth setting.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
